description: FIRST REAL-INCIDENT TRACE (from the predecessor, AUDIT-003). A fall followed by machine-like stillness and no response. The predecessor's Table-Mode/stillness veto CANCELLED a real detected fall 27s after impact (MotionManager field note) - an unconscious elder's still wrist matched its 'watch on a table' profile. In the new architecture this exact context must NEVER suppress escalation (D17 veto contract; the RFC-003 litmus): expects SUSPECTED -> COUNTDOWN -> ALERTING -> ESCALATING with zero suppression.

provenance.source: AUDIT-003 appendix, sub-report 1 (watch safety path): predecessor MotionManager.swift:2585-2593 stillness veto + ThreatDetectionManager.swift:1441-1444 GATE 1; field note of a real fall cancelled 27s post-impact (MonitoringCoordinator.swift:853-856)
provenance.whyExists: The exact failure shape the predecessor shipped: post-fall stillness reads as 'not a person' and vetoes the alert. The new pipeline must treat stillness after impact as corroboration, never exculpation.
provenance.whatItGuards: D17 corollary (context may never independently suppress a life-critical event) / H-01 / seeds VV-102 veto suite; RFC-003 evidence
